servinfo.title: Oil Pump Assembly Disassembly and Reassembly

ptxt.intxrefpoff: Oil Pump Assembly Removal and Installation

s1.ptxt: Remove rotor plate (1) by removing rotor plate mounting bolts (2).
Using quick-drying ink pen, make matchmark (4) on outer rotor and inner rotor as shown in figure.
Remove outer rotor (1) and inner rotor (2).

attention3.ptxt: A matchmark (3) is supposed to be up-pointing. Put a line mark (4) to help engage in the original position of each rotor when reinstalling.

s1.ptxt: Remove relief valve (1) and spring (2) by removing retainer (3) from rotor plate (4).
Wash clean and then dry all disassembled parts.
Apply thin coat of engine oil to the following parts.

item.ptxt: Inside surfaces of oil pump case.

s1.ptxt: Align match marks (4) on outer rotor and inner rotor as shown in figure.
Install outer rotor (1) and inner rotor (2) to oil pump case.

attention3.ptxt: When installing the outer rotor (1) and the inner rotor (2), face the matchmark (3) upward and align the line marks (4) to each other.

s1.ptxt: Apply engine oil to relief valve (1) and spring (2), and install them to rotor plate (4).
Install rotor plate and tighten rotor plate bolts to specified torque. After installing plate, check that rotors can be turned smoothly by hand (Torque: 0.3 N·m (0.03 kgf-m, 0.25 lbf-ft) or less).
